primary_region: Maharashtra, India (Solapur, Nashik, Pune, Sangli, Osmanabad belts)

Healthy.visual_symptoms: Firm bright red/green-red fruit with intact calyx. No spots, cracks, or water-soaked areas. Glossy blemish-free skin.
immediate_actions: Apply balanced NPK as per growth stage
do_not: Overhead irrigation — wets foliage and promotes fungal spread
watch_for: Any cracking which creates entry points for Alternaria
Healthy.timeline: No intervention needed. Monitor weekly during monsoon season.

Bacterial Blight.visual_symptoms: Water-soaked irregular spots on leaves with yellow halo turning necrotic brown/black. Fruit shows water-soaked lesions with L/Y/star-shaped cracks. Stems show dark water-soaked streaks with oozing in humid conditions.
Bacterial Blight.favourable_conditions: 25–33°C, RH >60%, monsoon rainfall June–September. Wind-driven rain splash spreads pathogen. Most destructive in Solapur belt.
Bacterial Blight.severity_note: Most economically destructive pomegranate disease in Maharashtra. Unmanaged orchards see 60–80% losses.
immediate_actions: Spray Streptocyclin 500 ppm + Copper Oxychloride 0.3% immediately
Repeat spray every 10–15 days for 2–3 rounds
Apply Bordeaux paste on all cut/pruned ends
Apply bleaching powder 20–25 kg/ha at plant base
Remove and burn all infected plant material
do_not: Overhead irrigation — spreads bacteria via water splash
Reuse pruning tools without disinfecting with bleach or Dettol
watch_for: New water-soaked spots appearing within 5–7 days of treatment
Bacterial Blight.timeline: With treatment, new infections halt in 10–14 days. Existing lesions do not reverse. Expect 3–4 spray cycles through monsoon.

Anthracnose.visual_symptoms: Small dark brown water-soaked lesions near calyx → depressed dark brown/black sunken spots with grey-orange spore masses. Leaves show dull violet-black dots with yellow patches and drop. Twig cankers with sunken tissue.
Anthracnose.favourable_conditions: 25–30°C, RH 50–80%, frequent rainfall. Peak: August–September kharif season. Fungus inactive in dry weather.
Anthracnose.severity_note: Up to 50% losses in Maharashtra (2020–21). Bhagawa cultivar highly susceptible.
immediate_actions: Spray Carbendazim 0.1% or Thiophanate methyl 0.1% at 15-day intervals for 2–3 rounds
Prune all affected twigs and apply copper paste on cuts
do_not: Delay in removing infected fruits — they become spore sources
watch_for: Twig die-back extending toward main branches
preventive_measures: First preventive spray at flowering onset
Rotate fungicide MOA classes (DMI, MBC, strobilurin) to prevent resistance
Anthracnose.timeline: Active sporulation stops within 7–10 days of correct fungicide. Full recovery of affected twigs: 3–4 weeks.

Cercospora Fruit Spot.visual_symptoms: Small yellowish spots with halo on sepals and fruit surface → blackish patches with dull white fungal crust, corky texture. Leaves: circular dark reddish-brown to black spots (0.5–5 mm) with yellow margins → yellowing and drop. Twig black elliptical depressed spots → die-back.
Cercospora Fruit Spot.favourable_conditions: High humidity, rainy conditions, water-saturated soil. Wind-borne spore spread. Warm humid summers.
immediate_actions: Spray Hexaconazole (Contaf) 0.1% or Carbendazim 0.1% at 15-day intervals for 2–3 rounds
Remove and destroy all diseased fruits and twigs
do_not: Delay removal of diseased material — wind spreads spores to healthy fruit
watch_for: Corky black patches spreading to larger fruit surface area
Cercospora Fruit Spot.timeline: Lesions do not heal. New infection stops in 10–14 days with treatment. Cosmetic damage remains — affects market value.

Alternaria Fruit Spot.causal_agent: Alternaria alternata and related Alternaria spp.
Alternaria Fruit Spot.visual_symptoms: Black Spot form: small reddish-brown/black spots (1–3 mm) with yellow halo → expand and merge, can cover >50% of surface. Heart Rot form: exterior looks normal but internal arils show brown rot — hollow sound when knocked. Leaves: chlorotic spots → premature drop.
Alternaria Fruit Spot.favourable_conditions: 20–35°C, RH >85%, post-monsoon. Fruit cracking from irregular irrigation is the primary entry point.
Alternaria Fruit Spot.severity_note: Heart Rot form is visually silent until harvest — impossible to detect externally. Bhagawa cultivar severely affected in Solapur belt.
immediate_actions: Spray Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole fungicide immediately
Ensure strict uniform drip irrigation to prevent fruit cracking
do_not: Irregular irrigation — fruit cracking is the main entry point for the pathogen
watch_for: New black spots appearing on fruit surface within 7 days
Any fruit cracking — treat immediately as entry point
For Heart Rot: soft or hollow-sounding fruits at harvest
preventive_measures: Uniform drip irrigation schedule — most important prevention
Alternaria Fruit Spot.timeline: Surface spots stop spreading in 7–10 days with treatment. Heart Rot is undetectable until harvest — prevention via irrigation management is the only control.
